title: Nucleic Acid Extraction, Amplification and Library Construction for Viral Metagenomic Sequencing.
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"24srh","text":" This protocol is a continuation of the previous protocol (dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.m4yc8xw) for viral metagenomic researches with the tissues of Molluscs, such as abalone, oyster, clam,  scallop and so on. This maybe also applicable for other animals, such as shrimp and fish, based on the principle of the protocol. But we havn't verified this idea yet.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":59,"length":40}],"data":[]},{"key":"27ifn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"6A97C9ADF7EF4CC199CE2AD998E2BAB5","url":"https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.m4yc8xw"}}}}

steps: Now, the supernatant from step 13 of below protocol is ready, We will use HP Viral DNA/RNA Kit (Omega Bio-Tek, Norcross, GA, USA) to extract both the viral DNA and RNA sitimutaniously. Isolation of Viral Like Particles (VLP) from Tissues of Molluscs by Jing-Zhe (Ginger) Jiang ♠ The kit HP Viral DNA/RNA Kit is not indispensable. You can replace it with any brand of kits, such as QIAamp cador Pathogen Mini Kit (Qiagen), as long as it suitable for extraction both viral DNA and RNA from liquid samples. ♣ The entire procedure MUST BE RNase free
Follow the kit protocol, then you will acquire 15-50 μl of viral DNA/RNA solution. DO NOT add carrier RNA, as carrier RNA will account a high percentage in the dataset from high throughput seqencing.

Use Qubit™ (Thermo Fisher) and Qubit™ dsDNA HS Assay Kit (Thermo Fisher) to specifically quantify the concentration of dsDNA. If the amount is high that means previous extraction is successful. If you like, you can also use Qubit™ RNA HS Assay Kit to specifically quantify RNA. But we don't recommand Qubit™ ssDNA Assay Kit, as it is not specific.

Now, we are ready to use REPLI-g® Cell WGA & WTA Kit (Qiagen) to amplify both viral DNA RNA, or only viral RNA. ♠ We havn't fully assessed the performances of three amplification strategies. ♣ However, we found that the production of WTA is usually lower than WGA and RT-WGA protocol. Sometime, the barren production of WTA will even not be qualified to the further library construction. ♥ And the differences in terms of viral diversity with three strategies are still under assessment. ♦ We just use half of the required volume of kit protocol to save some money. ◊ DO READ THROUGH the whole REPLI-g Cell WGA & WTA Handbook REPLI-g Cell WGA & WTA Qiagen Catalog #150052
Place 3 μl nucleic acid, add 2 μl Lysis Buffer. Mix by vortexing and centrifuge briefly.
Incubate at 24°C for 5 min. 00:05:00 Undetermined
Add 1 μl gDNA Wipeout Buffer the lysed sample. Mix by vortexing and centrifuge briefly.
Incubate at 42°C for 10 min. 00:10:00 Undetermined, Wiping out DNA

Prepare Quantiscript RT mix (Table 1). Table 1. Quantiscript RT mix %s %s ♠ gDNA Wipeout buffer will be deactivate in this Mix. So the DNA is kept during RT reaction. ♣ The using of both random and oligo dT Primers will improve the proformance of RT reaction. ♥ NOTE: The Quantiscript RT mix must be prepared fresh.
Add 4 μl RT mix to the aliquot (6 μl) in step 8. Mix by vortexing and centrifuge briefly.
Incubate at 42°C for 60 min 01:00:00 Undetermined, Reverse Transcripting
Stop the reaction by incubating at 95ºC for 3 min, then cool on ice. 00:03:00 Undetermined, Stopping RT Undetermined, Cooling

Prepare the ligation mix (Table 2). Table 2 Ligation mix %s %s IMPORTANT: When preparing the ligation mix, add the components in the order shown in Table 2. NOTE : The ligation mix must be prepared fresh.
Add 5 μl ligation mix to the WTA ready reaction (10 μl) from step 12. Mix by vortexing and centrifuge briefly.
Incubate at 24°C for 30 min. 00:30:00 Undetermined, Ligating
Stop the reaction by incubating at 95°C for 5 min. 00:05:00 Undetermined, Stopping ligating
Prepare REPLI-g SensiPhi amplification mix (Table 3). Table 3 Preparing REPLI-g Sensiphi amplification mix %s %s NOTE : The REPLI-g SensiPhi amplification mix must be prepared fresh.
Add 15 μl REPLI-g SensiPhi amplification mix to the ligation reaction (15 μl) from step 16. Mix by vortexing and centrifuge briefly.
Incubate at 30°C for 2h. 02:00:00 Undetermined, Amplifying

Stop the reaction by incubating at 65°C for 5 min. 00:05:00 Undetermined, Stopping Amplifying ♠ If not being used directly, store the amplified DNA at –15 ºC to –30ºC until required for downstream applications. ♣ We recommend storage of the amplified DNA at a concentration of at least 100 ng/μl.
Adjust the volume to 100 μl by adding the appropriate volume of TE buffer. Then equilibrate to room temperature (15–25°C) for no longer than 10 min. 00:10:00, RT equilibrating

Add Fully mixed 0.6 × volume of AMPure Beads (Beckman Coulter) to the solution from above step. Mix by gently pipetting entire volume up and down 10 times ♠ Take out AMPure Beads from refrigerator to sit in RT for at least 15 min. ♣ Make sure AMPure Beads is full mixed before use, this will ensure the purification effect.
Incubate at RT for 15 min. 00:15:00

Place the tube on an appropriate magnetic stand to separate beads from supernatant. After the solution is clear (about 5 minutes), carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads that contain DNA targets (Caution: do not discard beads). 00:05:00
Add 200-600 μl of freshly prepared 80% ethanol to the tube while in the magnetic stand. Incubate at room temperature for 30 seconds, and then carefully remove and discard the supernatant. 00:00:30
Repeat above Step to do the ethanol clean again.
Put the tube back in the magnetic stand,completely remove the residual ethanol, and air dry beads for 10 minutes while the tube is on the magnetic stand with lid open. 00:10:00
Elute the DNA target from the beads with 15 μl nuclease-free water. Mix well on a vortex mixer or by pipetting up and down, and put the tube in the magnetic stand until the solution is clear.
Transfer the supernatant to a clean PCR tube.

We recommend using the transposase base library construction strategy. These kit convert the complex steps of DNA fragment, end repair and adaptor connection into a one-step enzymatic reaction, which significantly reduce the demanded amount of the initial DNA and shorten the time of library construction. NEBNext® Ultra™ II FS DNA Library Prep Kit New England Biolabs Catalog #E7805, E6177 NEBNext Singleplex or Multiplex Oligos for Illumina New England Biolabs Catalog #E7350, E7335, E7500, E6609 TruePrep DNA Library Prep Kit V2 for Illumina Vazyme Catalog #TD501,502,503 TruePrep Index Kit V# for Illumina Vazyme Catalog #TD202-TD207
Place 3 μl nucleic acid, add 2 μl Lysis Buffer. Mix by vortexing and centrifuge briefly.
Incubate at 24°C for 5 min 00:05:00 Undetermined, Lysising

Preparing Quantiscript RT mix (Table 1): Table 1. Quantiscript RT mix %s %s ♠ gDNA Wipeout buffer will be deactivate in this Mix. So the DNA is kept during RT reaction. ♣ The using of both random and oligo dT Primers will improve the proformance of RT reaction. ♥ NOTE: The Quantiscript RT mix must be prepared fresh.
Add 5 μl RT mix to the aliquot (5 μl) in step 6. Mix by vortexing and centrifuge briefly.
Incubate at 42°C for 60 min 01:00:00 Undetermined, Reverse Transcripting
Stop the reaction by incubating at 95°C for 3 min, then cool on ice. 00:03:00 Undetermined, Stopping RT

Prepare the ligation mix (Table 2). Table 2 Ligation mix A B 1 Component Volume/reaction 2 Ligase Buffer 4 μl 3 Ligase Mix 1 μl 4 Total volume 5 μl IMPORTANT: When preparing the ligation mix, add the components in the order shown in Table 2. NOTE : The ligation mix must be prepared fresh.
Add 5 μl ligation mix to the WTA ready reaction (10 μl) from step 10. Mix by vortexing and centrifuge briefly.
Incubate at 24°C for 30 min. 00:30:00 Undetermined, Ligation
Stop the reaction by incubating at 95°C for 5 min,then cool on ice. 00:05:00 Undetermined, Stopping Ligation
Prepare REPLI-g SensiPhi amplification mix (Table 3). Table 3 Preparing REPLI-g Sensiphi amplification mix %s %s NOTE : The REPLI-g SensiPhi amplification mix must be prepared fresh.
Add 15 μl REPLI-g SensiPhi amplification mix to the ligation reaction (15 μl) from step 14. Mix by vortexing and centrifuge briefly.
Incubate at 30°C for 2h to amplify DNA and cDNA 02:00:00 Undetermined, Amplifying

Stop the reaction by incubating at 65°C for 5 min. 00:05:00 Undetermined, Stopping Amplifying ♠ If not being used directly, store the amplified DNA at –15 ºC to –30ºC until required for downstream applications. ♣ We recommend storage of the amplified DNA at a concentration of at least 100 ng/μl.
Adjust the volume to 100 μl by adding the appropriate volume of TE buffer. Then equilibrate to room temperature (15–25°C) for no longer than 10 min. 00:10:00, RT equilibratign

Add Fully mixed 0.6 × volume of AMPure Beads (Beckman Coulter) to the solution from above step. Mix by gently pipetting entire volume up and down 10 times ♠ Take out AMPure Beads from refrigerator to sit in RT for at least 15 min. ♣ Make sure AMPure Beads is full mixed before use, this will ensure the purification effect.
Incubate at RT for 15 min. 00:15:00, Incubating

Place the tube on an appropriate magnetic stand to separate beads from supernatant. After the solution is clear (about 5 minutes), carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads that contain DNA targets (Caution: do not discard beads). 00:05:00, Incubating

Add 200-600 μl of freshly prepared 80% ethanol to the tube while in the magnetic stand. Incubate at room temperature for 30 seconds, and then carefully remove and discard the supernatant. 00:00:30, Incubating
Repeat above Step to do the ethanol clean again.
Put the tube back in the magnetic stand,completely remove the residual ethanol, and air dry beads for 10 minutes while the tube is on the magnetic stand with lid open. 00:10:00, Air Drying
Elute the DNA target from the beads with 15 μl nuclease-free water. Mix well on a vortex mixer or by pipetting up and down, and put the tube in the magnetic stand until the solution is clear.
Transfer the supernatant to a clean PCR tube.

We recommend using the transposase base library construction strategy. These kit convert the complex steps of DNA fragment, end repair and adaptor connection into a one-step enzymatic reaction, which significantly reduce the demanded amount of the initial DNA and shorten the time of library construction. NEBNext® Ultra™ II FS DNA Library Prep Kit New England Biolabs Catalog #E7805, E6177 TruePrep DNA Library Prep Kit V2 for Illumina Contributed by users Catalog #TD501,502,503 TruePrep Index Kit V# for Illumina Vazyme Catalog #TD202-TD207 NEBNext Singleplex or Multiplex Oligos for Illumina New England Biolabs Catalog #E7350, #E7335, #E7500, #E6609, #
Place 3 μl nucleic acid, add 2 μl Lysis Buffer. Mix by vortexing and centrifuge briefly.

Preparing Quantiscript RT mix (Table 1): Table 1. Quantiscript RT mix A B 1 Component Volume/reaction 2 RT/Polymerase Buffer 2 μl 3 gDNA Wipeout Buffer 1 μl 4 H 2 O sc 0.5 μl 5 Random Primer 0.5 μl 6 Oligo dT Primer 0.5 μl 7 Quantiscript RT Enzyme Mix 0.5 μl 8 Total volume 5 μl ♠ gDNA Wipeout buffer will be deactivate in this Mix. So the DNA is kept during RT reaction. ♥ NOTE: The Quantiscript RT mix must be prepared fresh.
Add 5 μl RT mix to the aliquot (5 μl) in step 6. Mix by vortexing and centrifuge briefly.
Incubate at 24°C for 5 min 00:05:00 Undetermined, Lysising

Stop the reaction by incubating at 95°C for 3 min, then cool on ice. 00:03:00 Undetermined, Stopping RT ♥ NOTE: Comparing to the RT-WGA case, the step of "incubating 60min at 42 °C" is skipped here to avoid reverse transcription and subsequent amplification of RNA .

Prepare the ligation mix (Table 2). Table 2 Ligation mix A B 1 Component Volume/reaction 2 Ligase Buffer 4 μl 3 Ligase Mix 1 μl 4 Total volume 5 μl IMPORTANT: When preparing the ligation mix, add the components in the order shown in Table 2. NOTE : The ligation mix must be prepared fresh.
Add 5 μl ligation mix to the WTA ready reaction (10 μl) from step 10. Mix by vortexing and centrifuge briefly.
Incubate at 24°C for 30 min. 00:30:00 Undetermined, Ligation
Stop the reaction by incubating at 95°C for 5 min,then cool on ice. 00:05:00 Undetermined, Stopping Ligation

Prepare REPLI-g SensiPhi amplification mix (Table 3). Table 3 Preparing REPLI-g Sensiphi amplification mix A B 1 Component Volume/reaction 2 REPLI-g sc Reaction Buffer 14.5 μl 3 REPLI-g Sensiphi DNA Polymerase 0.5 μl 4 Total volume 15 μl NOTE : The REPLI-g SensiPhi amplification mix must be prepared fresh.
Add 15 μl REPLI-g SensiPhi amplification mix to the ligation reaction (15 μl) from step 14. Mix by vortexing and centrifuge briefly.
Incubate at 30°C for 2h to amplify DNA and cDNA 02:00:00 Undetermined, Amplifying

Stop the reaction by incubating at 65°C for 5 min. 00:05:00 Undetermined, Stopping Amplifying ♠ If not being used directly, store the amplified DNA at –15 ºC to –30ºC until required for downstream applications. ♣ We recommend storage of the amplified DNA at a concentration of at least 100 ng/μl.
creator: Jing-Zhe (Ginger) Jiang
